quoteFirst: “Our intervals of repose were now very short, for at
quoteLast: , March 16th, another rush drove irresistibly on the larboard quarter and stern, and, forcing the ship ahead, raised her upon the ice. A chaotic ruin followed; our poor and cherished courtyard, its walls and arched doors, gallery, and well-trodden paths, were rent, and in some parts ploughed up like dust.
title: The Sea: Its Stirring Story of Adventure, Peril, & Heroism. Volume 3
author: Whymper, Frederick

quoteFirst: The shot fell a little ahead of the _Yoshino_, throwing up a tall column of white water. Admiral Ito, in his official report, notes that this first shot was fired at
quoteLast: . The range, as noted on the _Chen Yuen_, was 5,200 yards, or a little over three and a half miles. The heavy barbette and bow guns of the _Chen Yuen_ and other ships now joined in, but still the Japanese van squadron came on without replying.
title: Famous Men and Great Events of the Nineteenth Century

quoteFirst: It is now past midnight in Philadelphia,
quoteLast: ; at Bakersfield it is only 9.50 P. M., but many of our people are retiring, for it has been a day fraught with pleasure and excitement, wearing both on the mind and body, and we all need rest and plenty of it to prepare us for the approaching morrow.

quoteFirst: "And the next year a man came up from the city, and his friends were in the next room to help him if he called, and he didn't call, and they were afraid to startle him by knocking, so they got a ladder and peeped into the window at
quoteLast: , and he lay peacefully on the bed with his eyes closed and his hands stretched loosely out, and they thought it was a great joke that he should sleep through it, so they went home, and in the morning they found him in horrible convulsions, and he never recovered.
title: Whom the Gods Destroyed
author: Bacon, Josephine Daskam, 1876-1961

quoteFirst: Then look at the piquant shyness of its face when the two hands are over each other. Several attitudes imply “Make ready.” The “make ready” of
quoteLast: differs from the “make ready” of ten minutes to twelve, as youth differs from age. “Upward and onward” says twenty-five minutes to eleven. Mid-day or midnight expresses distinctly “It is done.
